description.para: Calls below the compile-time SYN_LOG_LEVEL are compiled out entirely.

briefdescription.para: Compile-time minimum log level (defaults to SYN_LOG_DEBUG).
Size of the log formatting buffer in bytes.

detaileddescription.para: Set to 0 in syn_config.h to disable timestamp prefix.

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* SYN_LogOutputFunc) (const char *str, size_t len)
memberdef.name: SYN_LogOutputFunc

detaileddescription.para: Set to 1 in syn_config.h to enable ANSI color codes.
The logging system calls this to emit formatted output. The string is NOT null-terminated at position len (it is within the buffer, but len is the exact number of chars to write).

parameterdescription.para: Function that writes log output (e.g., to UART).
Minimum runtime log level (messages below this are suppressed at runtime). Compile-time level still applies.

briefdescription.para: Change the runtime minimum log level.
Get the current runtime minimum log level.

detaileddescription.para: Normally called via the SYN_LOG_* macros rather than directly.

memberdef.argsstring: (SYN_LogLevel level, const char *tag, const char *fmt, va_list args)
memberdef.name: syn_log_va

briefdescription.para: Output a raw string with no formatting or prefix.

detaileddescription.para: Useful for printing hex dumps, tables, or other pre-formatted data that shouldn't get a timestamp/level prefix.

briefdescription.para: Hex dump utility — output a buffer as hex + ASCII.

detaileddescription.para: Provides printf-style logging with severity levels, per-module tags, optional timestamps, and ANSI color output. Logging calls below the compile-time minimum level are stripped entirely (zero overhead).
